data.body:
PASS 1 plan-review (T10 — Infrastructure / IaC overlay), focused on the LAST checklist item: human/admin authentication to every network-reachable service the plan stands up. Overlay APPLIES (Terraform-provisioned cloud infra + an internet-facing Gerrit code-review server). ONE grounded finding.

FINDING (1):
- [ ] Specify how HUMAN principals (developers AND admins) authenticate to the internet-facing Gerrit web UI/REST (:443) and Gerrit SSH (:29418) — a named identity mechanism (OAuth/OIDC/SSO/LDAP/HTTP-auth) OR an explicit, justified no-auth rationale safe for an internet-exposed box. As written, the plan stands up Gerrit on a public surface (network.tf: 443 and 29418 ingress cidr_blocks=["0.0.0.0/0"], public EIP + DNS rebar.solutions.navateam.com) but the ONLY configured auth mode is auth.type=DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT (gerrit.config:75), under which any anonymous visitor can "become ANY account" incl. the Administrators group (first dev-login account 1000000 is auto-added to Administrators — bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh:15). That is not authentication; it is anonymous admin assumption reachable from the whole internet, which also defeats the plan's core integrity claim (the LLM-Review gate/submit-req can be self-voted/bypassed by anyone who "becomes" a Service User/admin). The plan's own config comment flags this as unresolved ("PoC ONLY ... do NOT leave this on a box that holds anything sensitive ... S3/S4a may refine to a real auth type"), and docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md:219 confirms the live box still runs DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT. No AC/ADR/plan section names a human-auth mechanism. Machine credentials (deploy key, bot HTTP token, SSM secrets) authenticate MACHINES, not human/admin access — per T10 they do not satisfy this sub-check. This is a deny-by-default under-specified (here, affirmatively unsafe) human-auth contract on an internet-reachable service.
  - criteria: [T10]
  - location: AC1 ("reverse proxy on 443; Gerrit HTTP 8080 + SSH 29418"); infra/terraform/network.tf:27-55; infra/compose/gerrit.config:69-75; infra/scripts/bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh:15,34-37; docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md:219
  - evidence: code fact — auth.type=DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT is the ONLY configured Gerrit auth mode (gerrit.config:75); network.tf opens 443+29418 to 0.0.0.0/0 with public EIP+DNS; first dev-login auto-added to Administrators (bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh:15) -> any anonymous internet visitor can assume admin; plan/config phrase "S3/S4a MAY refine to a real auth type ... do NOT leave this on a box that holds anything sensitive" = explicitly deferred; ABSENCE of any named human-auth mechanism (OAuth/OIDC/SSO/LDAP/HTTP-auth) in any AC/ADR; T10 last checklist item (human+admin auth on every stood-up network-reachable service; service-to-service creds do NOT count).
  - suggested_fix: Add an AC + ADR to S2 (or S3/S4a) pinning Gerrit's human-auth posture BEFORE the box is internet-exposed: EITHER (a) set auth.type to a real IdP (OAUTH via gerrit-oauth-provider with GitHub/Google OIDC, or HTTP behind an authenticating nginx layer) with anonymous write disabled; OR (b) if a real IdP is out of scope for the PoC, restrict the 443+29418 ingress cidr_blocks to a trusted operator CIDR/VPN/SG allowlist (not 0.0.0.0/0) and record that network-perimeter restriction as the explicit no-auth rationale — so DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT is never reachable from the open internet. State it in the plan rather than deferring with "S3/S4a may refine".

AFFIRMATIONS (T10 dimensions this plan satisfies — code-corroborated):
- (a) STATE + plan-before-apply: S3 remote backend with native use_lockfile locking (no local state, TF >= 1.10 floor), one-time local-state bootstrap for the state bucket; terraform plan-before-apply reaffirmed epic-wide (esp. the high-stakes S6 ruleset replace) and in S7's runbook AC.
- (b) LEAST-PRIVILEGE IAM: SSM read scoped to arn:...:parameter/rebar/prod/* (not *), kms:Decrypt bounded by kms:ViaService, single-owner role contract (S1 creates; S2/S4a only ADD scoped inline policies); replication deploy key single-repo write-only. No wildcard admin; no long-lived creds in IaC.
- (c) IDEMPOTENCY & DRIFT: single-owner contracts for the EC2 role, gerrit.config (S2 base + S3 [change]-only RMW) and the DLM policy (S1 owns; S7 only asserts); IaC-rendered configs, ignore_changes=[ami] to avoid churn.
- (d) BLAST RADIUS: prevent_destroy on the NoteDb data volume + EIP + state bucket; data volume separate from root; freeze-and-restore + "merged code safe on the GitHub mirror" bound RPO; single-env PoC explicitly accepted. (EBS at-rest encryption gap already raised in prior T5c/T10 findings — not duplicated here.)
- (f) COST & SIZING: single Graviton t4g.large, pinned ~2 GB heap, gp3, default VPC, no ALB/NAT, SSM (free) over Secrets Manager, DLM retain=7.
- (g) OBSERVABILITY & TEARDOWN: Rebar/Gate:GerritReachable metric + alarm, EC2 status-check backstops, SNS->SSM endpoint; reproducible as-code with a restore drill; documented state-bucket teardown.

No other NEW T10 defects — the single gap is the unspecified (and currently unsafe) human/admin authentication to the internet-facing Gerrit service.
